entries.label: Is the oil up to the indicator on the dip stick?
At what hours is next oil service? (oil and filter to be changed every 100 hours)
Was OUTER filter inspected and free of trash and debris?
Hours The OUTER Filter Was Replaced:
Hours the OUTER Filter Should Be Replaced:
Is the INNER filter clean? (Blackish in color indicates a dirty filter)
Hours The INNER Filter Was Replaced:
Hours the INNER Filter Should Be Replaced:
Is coolant at the appropriate level in the reservoir?
Did you add coolant to the reservoir?
Is coolant up to the bottom of radiators neck (a few inches below cap)?
Did you add coolant to the radiator?
Was hood lifted and debris removed from engine block, radiator, and removable screen?
Is the engine and engine compartment clean?
Is the area beneath the seat clean?
Is the topside of the mowing deck clean?
Is hydraulic oil level within the checkered indicator?
If the level is LOW, was hydraulic oil added? (Note: Add to tank very slowly)
If the level is HIGH, was hydraulic oil siphoned out?
Is water observed in the separator?
Was water purged from fuel line?

Note: To purge water - Start Engine. Turn the pit cock counter clockwise (black wingnut at bottom of separator) and let water seep from pit cock. Hand tighten clockwise once water has purged from separator.
What is the recommended tire pressure for this particular mower?
What is the current tire pressure?
Was tire inflated/deflated to correct PSI?
Track Tension-Deflection (play) is to be between 1/4" and 3/4". Are the tracks within this range?
NOTE: Tracks outside of 1/4" to3/4" must be adjusted before mower can return to the field
Are blades and spindles free of debris?
Are all safety guards in place?
Were spindles and hubs greased (to be greased daily)
Is the mower having any issues?
